# Research cold-email drafts for Celina Lin

Review the employer posting and lab website before sending. Replace generic recipients with a specific PI or hiring manager when possible.

## UMass Chan Medical School — Research Associate I

**Subject:** Interest in Research Associate I — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear UMass Chan Medical School Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Research Associate I opportunity with UMass Chan Medical School at UMass Chan Medical School.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a42e4606c326942b4e848e5

---

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Celina Lin

Dear SS&C Technologies Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Associate Learning & Development Specialist - Healthcare Call Center opportunity with SS&C Technologies at SS&C Technologies.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a2c47987cd40a338fae59df

---

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. ## Virginia Commonwealth University School of Medicine — Research Assistant

**Subject:** Interest in Research Assistant — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Virginia Commonwealth University School of Medicine Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Research Assistant opportunity with Virginia Commonwealth University School of Medicine at Virginia Commonwealth University School of Medicine.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. **Subject:** Interest in Campus Marketing Representative (University of Michigan) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Martin-Brower of Canada Co. Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Campus Marketing Representative (University of Michigan) opportunity with Martin-Brower of Canada Co. at Martin-Brower of Canada Co..

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Program Coordinator, Hart Leadership Program (HLP), Sanford School of Public Policy opportunity with Duke University at Duke University.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

## YMCA of Greater Houston — Youth Development Counselor - Wilkinson Elementary (Conroe)

**Subject:** Interest in Youth Development Counselor - Wilkinson Elementary (Conroe) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear YMCA of Greater Houston Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Youth Development Counselor - Wilkinson Elementary (Conroe) opportunity with YMCA of Greater Houston at YMCA of Greater Houston.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Clinical Research Coordinator A/B (Department of Neurology) opportunity with University of Pennsylvania at University of Pennsylvania.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

## Care Access — Clinical Research Coordinator I

**Subject:** Interest in Clinical Research Coordinator I — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Care Access Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Clinical Research Coordinator I opportunity with Care Access at Care Access.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a288fb2883c0d3be6542d9b

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Software Engineer – University Grad opportunity with Cylake at Cylake.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

## Centinela Hospital Medical Center — Customer Service Representative

**Subject:** Interest in Customer Service Representative — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Centinela Hospital Medical Center Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Customer Service Representative opportunity with Centinela Hospital Medical Center at Centinela Hospital Medical Center.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Client Service Represenative opportunity with Banfield Pet Hospital at Banfield Pet Hospital.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Supply Chain Associate (1st Shift) opportunity with University of Iowa at University of Iowa.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Celina Lin

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the After School Program Coordinator - Leesburg, FL opportunity with The Tree House Foundation at The Tree House Foundation.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

**Subject:** Interest in Kindergarten Para - 5.5 hours per day - West Willow — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Kindergarten Para - 5.5 hours per day - West Willow opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

Dear Dr. Rameen Beroukhim and Dr. Simona Dalin,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Bioinformatics Analyst opportunity with Beroukhim–Dalin computational genomics team at Dana-Farber Cancer Institute / Broad Institute.

I am especially interested in how genomic rearrangements and DNA-repair errors reveal cancer vulnerabilities. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my BRCA1/BRCA2 population-variant project gave me practical experience building reproducible variant-analysis workflows and thinking carefully about how genomic evidence is interpreted.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Daycare Certified Infant and Toddler Teachers opportunity with Little Sprouts, LLC at Little Sprouts, LLC.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. ## The Salvation Army of North Texas — Youth Development Professional (6744)

**Subject:** Interest in Youth Development Professional (6744) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear The Salvation Army of North Texas Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Youth Development Professional (6744) opportunity with The Salvation Army of North Texas at The Salvation Army of North Texas.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a465cc93dbab558e29a6107

**Subject:** Interest in BILINGUAL RESOURCE FACILITATOR 1 / Probationary / 3.75 HRS / Conestoga Middle School (Multilingual Department) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the BILINGUAL RESOURCE FACILITATOR 1 / Probationary / 3.75 HRS / Conestoga Middle School (Multilingual Department) opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

**Subject:** Interest in Nappanee KidsCare Youth Development Professional - AM — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Boys & Girls Clubs of Elkhart County Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Nappanee KidsCare Youth Development Professional - AM opportunity with Boys & Girls Clubs of Elkhart County at Boys & Girls Clubs of Elkhart County.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

**Subject:** Interest in Interim Teacher (non-certified) - Special Education (Districtwide) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Interim Teacher (non-certified) - Special Education (Districtwide) opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a45b2c9c2d11a6a46669e94

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

**Subject:** Interest in Child Development Instructional Aide, West Bishop Preschool- Bishop, CA — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear East Side Union High School District Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Child Development Instructional Aide, West Bishop Preschool- Bishop, CA opportunity with East Side Union High School District at East Side Union High School District.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

**Subject:** Interest in (2026-2027 School Year) Teacher - Elementary (1st grade) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the (2026-2027 School Year) Teacher - Elementary (1st grade) opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a44290ab156014e414bbb5e

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

**Subject:** Interest in Tutors & TCAT Interns (26/27) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Clarksville Montgomery County School System Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Tutors & TCAT Interns (26/27) opportunity with Clarksville Montgomery County School System at Clarksville Montgomery County School System.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/6a44a61365e80d3c99f2d024

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the SCFP-CUPE - Fall 2026 - TA - CRM3301 B00 opportunity with [uOttawa at [uOttawa.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. ## SchoolSpring — CAFETERIA MONITOR

**Subject:** Interest in CAFETERIA MONITOR — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear SchoolSpring Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the CAFETERIA MONITOR opportunity with SchoolSpring at SchoolSpring.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

## Responsive Education Solutions — Substitute Teaching Pool - Founders Classical Academy of Prosper

**Subject:** Interest in Substitute Teaching Pool - Founders Classical Academy of Prosper — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Responsive Education Solutions Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Substitute Teaching Pool - Founders Classical Academy of Prosper opportunity with Responsive Education Solutions at Responsive Education Solutions.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

I am reaching out about the Instructional Specialist - PE / 22 hrs wk / SHORT TERM - Lupin Hill Elementary School - Testing: Thursdays at 9:00 am (except holidays) at the District Office opportunity with East Side Union High School District at East Side Union High School District.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. ## Banfield Pet Hospital — Client Service Coordinator - 005168

**Subject:** Interest in Client Service Coordinator - 005168 — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Banfield Pet Hospital Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Client Service Coordinator - 005168 opportunity with Banfield Pet Hospital at Banfield Pet Hospital.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Client Service Coordinator Full Time opportunity with Banfield Pet Hospital at Banfield Pet Hospital.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://jobright.ai/jobs/info/69f8273a582f810f9c8ccf9e

In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in bioinformatics, pipeline development, and an LLM-assisted competency-mapping project has made me especially interested in rigorous, interpretable AI for biomedical data.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in bioinformatics, pipeline development, and an LLM-assisted competency-mapping project has made me especially interested in rigorous, interpretable AI for biomedical data.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the {Sr/Staff) Field Applications Scientist opportunity with Illumina at Illumina.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

## Genentech / Roche — Purification Development Scientist (Philadelphia)

**Subject:** Interest in Purification Development Scientist (Philadelphia) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Genentech / Roche Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Purification Development Scientist (Philadelphia) opportunity with Genentech / Roche at Genentech / Roche.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

**Subject:** Interest in Associate Systems Analyst - SM LIMS — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Amgen Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Associate Systems Analyst - SM LIMS opportunity with Amgen at Amgen.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/natera/jobs/6101779004

**Subject:** Interest in Associate Clinical Oncology Specialist — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Natera Hiring Team,

I am especially interested in building scalable data systems that let research and clinical teams use biological data reliably. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. ## 10x Genomics — District Sales Manager, Gulf Coast

**Subject:** Interest in District Sales Manager, Gulf Coast — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear 10x Genomics Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the District Sales Manager, Gulf Coast opportunity with 10x Genomics at 10x Genomics.

I am especially interested in the lab's use of single-cell and spatial transcriptomic data to connect molecular mechanisms with therapeutic questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, I have built reproducible genomics workflows and enjoy translating complex biological questions into clear, testable computational analyses.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, I have built API-driven and Snakemake-based pipelines, automated SQL workflows, and worked across both scientific and software contexts.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://roche.wd3.myworkdayjobs.com/ROG-A2O-GENE/job/South-San-Francisco/Research---Early-Development-Enterprise-Architect_202605-112708-1

## Glean — Sales Development Representative (Spanish)

**Subject:** Interest in Sales Development Representative (Spanish) — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Glean Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Sales Development Representative (Spanish) opportunity with Glean at Glean.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

More broadly, I have built API-driven and Snakemake-based pipelines, automated SQL workflows, and worked across both scientific and software contexts.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

## Natera — Design Transfer Scientist

**Subject:** Interest in Design Transfer Scientist — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Natera Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Design Transfer Scientist opportunity with Natera at Natera.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Manager, FP&A opportunity with Freenome at Freenome.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/freenome/jobs/8428167002

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the PSC Manager - Las Vegas, NV opportunity with Natera at Natera.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
https://linkedin.com/in/celina-g-lin

Posting: https://job-boards.greenhouse.io/natera/jobs/6101951004

## Amgen — Data Engineer

**Subject:** Interest in Data Engineer — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Amgen Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Data Engineer opportunity with Amgen at Amgen.

I am especially interested in building scalable data systems that let research and clinical teams use biological data reliably. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, I have built API-driven and Snakemake-based pipelines, automated SQL workflows, and worked across both scientific and software contexts.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin

## Amgen — Sr Associate Quality Complaints

**Subject:** Interest in Sr Associate Quality Complaints — biology/bioinformatics graduate

Dear Amgen Hiring Team,

My name is Celina Lin, and I recently completed my Honours Bachelor of Science in Biology/Bioinformatics at the University of Waterloo. I am reaching out about the Sr Associate Quality Complaints opportunity with Amgen at Amgen.

I am especially interested in the team's use of quantitative and computational methods to answer meaningful biological questions. In my independent BRCA1/BRCA2 project, I built a reproducible Python and Snakemake workflow using BCFtools and ANNOVAR to analyze population-specific variants and examine discrepancies between clinical labels and observed allele frequencies. I also completed two bioinformatics internships at the Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, where I developed an LLM-assisted competency-mapping pipeline and created research-data-management learning resources. More broadly, my background in biology, bioinformatics, Python, R, SQL, Linux, and reproducible workflow development aligns well with that kind of cross-disciplinary work.

The combination of rigorous biological questions, collaborative research, and hands-on computational work in this role is exactly the environment in which I hope to grow. I would be grateful for a 15–20 minute conversation to learn more about the lab's current priorities and discuss where my background could be useful. I have attached my resume for context.

Best,
Celina Lin
